intro.title: EDA Mindset
intro.bodyMarkdown:
EDA Mindset is a core topic when learning R for data analysis, statistics, and reproducible research.

Practicing this topic builds fluency with R syntax, data structures, and the tidyverse ecosystem.

Run the sample code below, experiment in the editor, then work through the challenges.
intro.sandboxCode: print("Ask questions, visualize, iterate before modeling")

challenges.title: Easy: Use print
challenges.promptMarkdown: Write R code that uses `print` related to **EDA Mindset**.
challenges.starterCode: print("Ask questions, visualize, iterate before modeling")

tests.label: Correct output
tests.assertion: identical(trimws(_cq_stdout), "Ask questions, visualize, iterate before modeling")

challenges.title: Easy: Print a number
challenges.promptMarkdown: Print the number `100` using `print()`.

challenges.title: Easy: Assign a variable
challenges.promptMarkdown: Create variable `x` with `<-` and set it to `5`, then print `x`.

challenges.title: Medium: Apply EDA Mindset
challenges.promptMarkdown: Write code demonstrating **EDA Mindset**.
challenges.starterCode: print("Ask questions, visualize, iterate before modeling")

challenges.title: Medium: Two-step snippet
challenges.promptMarkdown: Use `print` and `print` in one script.

challenges.title: Medium: Vector basics
challenges.promptMarkdown: Create `v <- c(1, 2, 3)` and print `sum(v)`.

challenges.title: Hard: Conditional output
challenges.promptMarkdown: Set `n <- 10`. If `n > 0`, print `"positive"`.

challenges.title: Hard: Function call
challenges.promptMarkdown: Use `length()` on `c(4, 8, 15)` and print the result.

challenges.title: Expert: Topic practice
challenges.promptMarkdown: Combine ideas from **EDA Mindset** in a short script using `print`.

challenges.title: Expert: Multi-line script
challenges.promptMarkdown: Write at least two lines of R related to **EDA Mindset** (include `print`).
challenges.starterCode: print("Ask questions, visualize, iterate before modeling")

questions.prompt: EDA primarily helps you:
Which function prints output in R?
R is widely used for:
What assignment operator is preferred in R?
Missing values in R are represented by:
Which function combines values into a vector?
Data frames are:
Type the function to get help on a topic.
Expert: Vectorized operations in R:
Expert: Name one keyword from EDA Mindset.

mtcars %>% group_by(cyl) %>% summarize(m = mean(mpg), sd = sd(mpg))

challenges.title: Medium: Vector basics
challenges.promptMarkdown: Create `v <- c(1, 2, 3)` and print `sum(v)`.

challenges.title: Hard: Conditional output
challenges.promptMarkdown: Set `n <- 10`. If `n > 0`, print `"positive"`.

challenges.title: Hard: Function call
challenges.promptMarkdown: Use `length()` on `c(4, 8, 15)` and print the result.

challenges.title: Expert: Topic practice
challenges.promptMarkdown: Combine ideas from **Common EDA Pitfalls** in a short script using `print`.

challenges.title: Expert: Multi-line script
challenges.promptMarkdown: Write at least two lines of R related to **Common EDA Pitfalls** (include `print`).

questions.prompt: A common EDA mistake is:

choices.label: Only looking for patterns you expect

R is widely used for:
